generated_oml:
instance MabiMax100IndustrialRobot : DTDFVocab:PhysicalSystem [
    base:desc "The MABI MAX 100 industrial robot, a specialized precision machining system comprising motors, gearboxes, and control mechanisms operating in the manufacturing domain for high-accuracy tasks."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MabiMax100IndustrialRobot
]

instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motors for robotic axes with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controllers for real-time control, constrained by ±190° azimuth movement with max speed |q̇max| = 75°/s."
]

instance Gearbox : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearboxes interfacing motors and robotic axes with safety constraints enforced by mechanical stops and software safeguards."
]

instance SafetyCell : DTDFVocab:SafetyComponent [
    base:desc "Physical safety cell limiting robot movement to ±190° azimuth with emergency stop protocols for deterministic actuation coordination."
]

instance ControlInterface : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded control systems (PLC-based loops) enabling remote control via Ethernet/IP or fieldbus for distributed actuation coordination."
]

instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Directly mounted encoder on the motor providing high-resolution angular position feedback (encoder counts per revolution) for closed-loop control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorDataStream
]

instance SecondaryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Redundant encoder located behind the gearbox for position feedback, ensuring 1° error for precision machining tasks."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataStream
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor for temperature measurement (time-series data) aggregated into contextual traces for manufacturing monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataStream
]

instance PressureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor for pressure measurement (time-series data) aggregated into contextual traces for manufacturing monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PressureDataStream
]

instance VibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor for vibration measurement (time-series data) aggregated into contextual traces for manufacturing monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VibrationDataStream
]

instance MotorDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
    DTDFVocab:protocol MQTT
    DTDFVocab:dataType TimeSeriesData
    DTDFVocab:frequency HighFrequency
    DTDFVocab:transmissionRate RealTime
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryPositionEncoder
    DTDFVocab:protocol MQTT
    DTDFVocab:dataType TimeSeriesData
    DTDFVocab:frequency HighFrequency
    DTDFVocab:transmissionRate RealTime
]

instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
    DTDFVocab:protocol MQTT
    DTDFVocab:dataType TimeSeriesData
    DTDFVocab:frequency Continuous
    DTDFVocab:transmissionRate LowLatency
]

instance PressureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PressureSensor
    DTDFVocab:protocol MQTT
    DTDFVocab:dataType TimeSeriesData
    DTDFVocab:frequency Continuous
    DTDFVocab:transmissionRate LowLatency
]

instance VibrationDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VibrationSensor
    DTDFVocab:protocol MQTT
    DTDFVocab:dataType TimeSeriesData
    DTDFVocab:frequency Continuous
    DTDFVocab:transmissionRate LowLatency
]

instance DigitalShadowContext : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Passive, context-aware data repository aggregating time-series sensor data (temperature, pressure, vibration) from physical system into contextual traces for manufacturing monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorDataStream, SecondaryEncoderDataStream, TemperatureDataStream, PressureDataStream, VibrationDataStream
    DTDFVocab:aggregationMethod AutomatedPipeline
    DTDFVocab:purpose ManufacturingMonitoring
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Functional architecture model of MABI MAX 100 robot decomposed into functional components using SysML Interaction Blocks Diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance LogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Logical architecture model capturing system behavior decomposition across requirements, functions, logic, and physics layers using MontiArc Automata."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcTool
]

instance TechnicalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Technical layer model describing component interfaces and interconnections (AADL/ACME) for deterministic actuation coordination."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AADLTool
]

instance DigitalShadowReference : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow reference model implementing Kritzinger et al. framework for one-way automated data aggregation from physical sensors via UML class diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UMLTool
]

instance SimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model using MathWorks Simulink for dynamic behavior representation of robot actuators and control systems."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for lightweight pub-sub sensor data streaming with QoS levels for reliability in real-time manufacturing monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowContext
]

instance DigitalShadowSoftware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software components interpreting incoming MQTT data streams to aggregate contextual traces for passive digital shadow representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowContext
]

instance MBSEFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Based Systems Engineering framework (SysML, MontiArc, AADL) enabling hierarchical abstraction modeling across requirements, functional, logical, and technical layers."
    DTDFVocab:enables FunctionalArchitectureModel, LogicalArchitectureModel, TechnicalModel
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine for dynamic behavior modeling and validation of robot control systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationModel
]

instance VersionControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous stakeholder integration tool compatibility framework for model evolution across SysML, MontiArc, and simulation tools."
    DTDFVocab:enables FunctionalArchitectureModel, LogicalArchitectureModel, SimulationModel
]

instance ManufacturingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Contextual performance analysis derived from aggregated time-series data traces (temperature, pressure, vibration) for precision machining quality assessment."
]

instance SafetyViolationAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic emergency response triggered by threshold crossings in sensor data (e.g., vibration exceeding safety limits) via PLC-based control systems."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous maintenance scheduling based on behavioral modeling (state machines) of gearbox and motor degradation patterns."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance QualityControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time quality assessment derived from position encoder synchronization and temperature/pressure stability analysis for machining accuracy."
]

instance OperationalEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance optimization recommendations derived from simulation-based analysis of control loop dynamics and actuator response times."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in this framework is defined through a **UML-based Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1), where digital shadows serve as **passive, one-way data aggregation entities** for contextual data traces (e.g., in manufacturing). The **orchestration** of the Digital Twin system is enabled by **active components** (e.g., software systems) that interpret and process incoming data from shadows, enabling autonomous behavior and bidirectional data flow. The **architecture patterns** likely rely on **component & connector perspectives** (e.g., ACME, AADL, MontiArc) to describe system composition, behavior (via automata or state machines in SysML), and integration with simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink). Resource management and system-wide coordination are handled through **model-based systems engineering (MBSE) methodologies** (e.g., SysML, RFLP), ensuring alignment across heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
A Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the described context is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, spanning **multi-level abstraction phases** (requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers). The lifecycle governance involves:
- **Requirements phase**: Defined via **SysML** or **ADLs** (e.g., SysML IBDs) to capture functional and logical specifications.
- **Functional/Logical Architecture phase**: Utilizes **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc**, or **SysML** to model components, interfaces, and interconnections, optionally augmented with **state machines** or **automata** (e.g., MontiArc Automaton) for behavior.
- **Simulation/Code phase**: Models are refined using **MathWorks Simulink**, other simulation tools, or general-purpose languages (**C++/Java**) for detailed behavior validation.
**Transition management** relies on **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **tool compatibility** (e.g., bridging SysML, MontiArc, and simulation tools). No explicit lifecycle stages or governance frameworks (e.g., CMMI, ISO 15288) are detailed, but the emphasis on **abstraction levels** and **methodological splits** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES) implies structured progression. **Lifecycle governance** implicitly involves **stakeholder alignment** and **model consistency** across tools and notations.

"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity and Validation:**
  - **Fidelity Requirements**: Not explicitly quantified in the documents; instead, the focus is on **model abstraction** (e.g., digital shadows as passive entities) and **data aggregation** for specific purposes (e.g., manufacturing).
  - **Validation/Verification Methods**: Implicitly addressed via **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) that ensure consistency across abstraction layers (requirements → behavior → architecture). No specific tools or protocols (e.g., formal verification) are mentioned.
  - **Uncertainty Sources**: Not detailed; the digital shadow’s role is passive, with no mention of error detection/correction mechanisms.
  - **Quality Assurance**: Relies on **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and **model tool compatibility** (e.g., Simulink, C++/Java) to maintain coherence across models.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  Implemented via **MQTT protocol** for sensor data streaming, with:
  - **Communication Protocol**: MQTT (broker-mediated, lightweight, and scalable for IoT).
  - **Network Topology**: Not specified, but implied to be **distributed** (given sensor decentralization in manufacturing contexts).
  - **Bandwidth/Latency**: Not quantified, but MQTT’s QoS levels (0–2) ensure low-latency (<100ms typical) for critical applications.
  - **Reliability/Redundancy**: Implicitly supported via MQTT QoS (e.g., QoS 1 for ordered delivery) and **digital shadow aggregation** (redundant data traces).
  - **Security Protocols**: Not mentioned; assumed to rely on **MQTT over TLS/SSL** (standard for IoT security) or embedded authentication (e.g., X.509 certificates).
  The system integrates with **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** tools (e.g., SysML, AADL) for cross-disciplinary validation.
 "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Not in Document
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external systems via Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**, a standardized interoperability framework for Industry 4.0 applications. Data exchange is facilitated through **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)**, a lightweight protocol enabling real-time streaming of contextual data traces between the physical and digital domains. Dependencies on external systems are implied through the need for **active components** to process incoming data, though no specific integration patterns with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems are detailed beyond these foundational protocols."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry-specific standards and methodologies** for modeling and interoperability:
  - **Modeling Languages**: Compliant with **SysML [17]**, **AADL [24]**, and **MontiArc** for architecture description.
  - **Simulation Tools**: Utilizes **MathWorks Simulink [28]** for behavioral modeling and validation.
  - **No Explicit Standards**: No references to ISO/IEC 19652 (Digital Twin standards), IEEE 1516 (Simulation), or certification frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC 27010) are mentioned. The documents focus on **methodological frameworks** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) rather than compliance frameworks.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system leverages the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework, as defined in the **Reference Architecture Model Industrie 4.0 (RAMI 4.0)**, to ensure security and safety compliance. The system incorporates **cybersecurity protocols** through the AAS's standardized interfaces, which enforce secure data exchange and access control via **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)** principles implicitly referenced in the **Asset Administration Shell** documentation (Document 50). The **safety cell** (as mentioned in Document 42) restricts the maximum working range of robotic systems (e.g., MABI robotic arms like the **Max-100-2-25-P**) to mitigate operational risks, ensuring fail-safe mechanisms are in place. While the documents do not explicitly detail **ISO 26262** compliance or formal risk assessment methodologies, the AAS’s structured modeling and conformance relations (Document 41) inherently support systematic safety validation through model-driven engineering (Document 43). The system’s **cyber-physical integration** aligns with RAMI 4.0 layers, ensuring safety-critical operations are isolated and monitored at the **Physical Layer** and **Cyber Layer** boundaries."
]
